IL HIGHWAY 115 WAS COVERED QUICKLY WITH WATER BASED ON FIELD DEBRIS AND MUD SIGNATURES OVER THE ROAD. MULTIPLE OTHER ROADS HAD SIGNATURES THEY HAD BEEN COVERED WITH WATER. BASED ON RADAR THIS LIKELY HAPPENED TWICE IN TWO HEAVY RAINFALL BOUTS SEPARATED BY SIX HOURS. THIS WAS THE FIRST TIME AND START TIME IS ESTIMATED PER RADAR.

AN NWS STORM SURVEY TEAM DETERMINED STRAIGHT-LINE WINDS TOPPLED APPROXIMATELY FIFTY 70-FOOT HIGH WOODEN UTILITY POLES. THESE WERE BLOWN DOWN TO THE EAST SOUTHEAST ACROSS A RAILROAD LINE IMMEDIATELY TO THE NORTH OF U.S. HIGHWAY 24 FOR A 3 MILE NARROW SWATH. SEVERAL WERE SNAPPED. A FEW LARGE TREES ALSO NEAR THE RAILROAD WERE DOWNED. TIME ESTIMATED BASED ON REPORTS AND RADAR.

NUMEROUS LARGE TREES DOWNED SCATTERED THROUGHOUT THE TOWN. DENSITY WAS THE HIGHEST ON THE NORTH SIDE OF TOWN...WHERE THREE UTILITY POLES WERE ALSO DOWNED. THERE WAS MINOR ROOF DAMAGE TO A FEW BUILDINGS. TIME ESTIMATED BASED ON ACCOUNTS AND RADAR.

2 REPORTS RCVD - EAST OF CRESCENT CITY TO WATSEKA ALONG US 24. EST 60 POWER POLES BLOWN DOWN OVER RR TRACKS IN A 3/4 TO 1 1/2 MILE SECTION. OTHER POLES WERE DAMAGED. TIME APPROX FROM 2140 UTC TO 2200 UTC

AN NWS STORM SURVEY TEAM DETERMINED AN EF1 TORNADO WITH MAXIMUM WINDS OF 85 TO 95 MPH TOUCHED DOWN 2.5 MILES ESE TO 4 MILES ESE OF WATSEKA WITH A PATH LENGTH OF APPROXIMATELY 1.5 MILES. MULTIPLE OUTBUILDINGS WERE DAMAGED AT TWO FARMSTEADS...INCLUDING ONE SMALL BARN WITH CINDER BLOCK WALLS BASICALLY DESTROYED WITH ITS ROOFED TOSSED ONE HALF MILE. A HANDFUL OF LARGE TREES ALSO WERE DOWNED.

DELAYED REPORT. TIME BASED OFF RADAR. POSSIBLE TORNADO. DAMAGE STARTED ON SE SIDE OF WATSEKA E OF RT 1 AND S OF RT 24. MULTIPLE TREES DOWN. SIDING RIPPED OFF BLDGS. SIGNS MANGLED. ON A PROPERTY ON CR 1730N...A 40X40 CINDER BLOCK BLDG WAS TORN APART...WITH CINDER BLOCKS TOSSED AND LIGHTER DEBRIS FROM BLDG TOSSED 1-1.5 MILES DOWNSTREAM TO THE NEXT PROPERTY. HERE...A HOUSE HAD WINDOWS BLOWN OUT AND A CAMPER INSIDE A GARAGE WAS IMPALED BY A BOARD. DAMAGE CONTINUED TO NEAR STATE LINE.
1730N ROAD BUCKLED AT THE INTERSECTION OF RTE 1. THIS WAS VERY LIKELY DUE TO WATER. TIME IS ESTIMATED.
DELAYED REPORT. TIME ESTIMATED FROM RADAR. 2 POWERPOLES DOWN AND A LARGE TREE LIMB ACROSS A ROAD.
